name: Martin Block
abstract: Martin Block (February 3, 1903 – September 18, 1967) was an American disc jockey. It is said that Walter Winchell invented the term "disc jockey" as a means of describing Block's radio work.

concerts.label: Look Magazine Amateur Swing Band Contest
Children's Asthma Research Institute and Hospital 3rd Annual Parade of Stars

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Barnet
about_quotes.evidence: I would be inclined to think that Martin Block had arrested the supremacy of the battle of the Make Believe Ballrooms by then.

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Jerome
about_quotes.evidence: Martin said, 'Well we’ve got a pretty good tenor man here, Jerry Jerome.' Benny said, 'Yeah, yeah, get your horn, sit in.'

about_quotes.speaker: Danny Barker
about_quotes.evidence: When I approached him in the (?) and asked him, 'Have you heard my wife?' He said, 'Yes, I have.'

about_quotes.speaker: Sol Yaged
about_quotes.evidence: I listened to a radio station all my life, for 50 years, WNEW, with a man called Martin Block.
